GOOGLE – $10B AI DATA CENTRE PROJECT IN VIZAG 🌐🤖📊

PROJECT OVERVIEW  
• Google to invest $10 billion in AI data centre project in Visakhapatnam  
• Groundbreaking scheduled for April 28 with Andhra Pradesh CM & Google officials  
• Among largest tech FDI investments in India  

INVESTMENT CONTEXT  
• Part of broader $15 billion cumulative India investment plan  
• Signals long-term commitment to India’s digital ecosystem  

INFRA SCALE  
• ~1 GW hyperscale data centre capacity planned  
• One of Google’s largest data centre projects in Asia  
• Built to support next-gen AI workloads  

LOCATION & CAMPUSES  
• Three campuses planned:  
  • Adavivaram  
  • Taraluvada  
  • Rambilli  
• Will anchor the proposed “AI City Vizag”  

TECHNOLOGY FOCUS  
• Deployment of full AI stack infrastructure  
• Supports cloud, AI models, and enterprise workloads  
• Enables large-scale compute + storage capabilities  

STRATEGIC IMPACT  
• Accelerates AI adoption across Indian enterprises  
• Boosts innovation ecosystem & digital transformation  
• Strengthens India’s position in global AI infra  

STATE-LEVEL PUSH  
• Aligns with Andhra Pradesh’s vision to become tech hub  
• Enhances data infrastructure & employment ecosystem  

CORE INSIGHT  
• Hyperscale AI infra becoming critical national asset  
• Google doubling down on India as key AI growth market  
• Large-scale compute investments to drive next tech cycle  
• Vizag emerging as major data centre & AI hub  

Impact: Positive for India’s digital infrastructure & AI ecosystem

text:
MEMORY CHIP SHORTAGE – PRICE PRESSURE BUILDS 💾📊

SHORTAGE OUTLOOK  
• Memory chip shortage to persist till end-2027  
• Highlighted by Asus Co-CEO Samson Hu  
• Described as a “critical and common challenge” across the ecosystem  

PRICING IMPACT  
• Product prices likely to increase across laptops & PCs  
• “Pricing increase has to be there” — management commentary  
• Already visible across global brands  
• India also seeing similar pricing trends  

DEMAND–SUPPLY DYNAMICS  
• Structural shortage driven by AI infrastructure demand  
• NAND & DRAM supply increasingly absorbed by AI servers  
• Multiple sectors competing for limited memory capacity  

GLOBAL VS INDIA IMPACT  
• Global sales decline: up to 15% in some regions  
• India relatively resilient: low single-digit impact  
• Domestic demand holding better vs global markets  

ASUS INDIA STRATEGY  
• Focus on maintaining growth momentum  
• Strong confidence in Indian market opportunity  
• Positioned to gain share amid global slowdown  

REVENUE MIX – INDIA  
• Majority revenue from enterprise laptop sales  
• Key sectors:  
  • Banking  
  • IT & ITeS  

BROADER INDUSTRY IMPACT  
• Price hikes spreading across:  
  • Laptops & PCs  
  • Smartphones  
  • Consumer durables (e.g., ACs)  
• Supply squeeze impacting entire electronics value chain  

STRUCTURAL TREND  
• Not a short-term disruption — multi-year imbalance  
• AI-led demand reshaping semiconductor allocation  
• Memory becoming a strategic bottleneck  

CORE INSIGHT  
• Memory shortage evolving into long-cycle constraint (~18+ months)  
• Sustained pricing pressure across electronics till 2027  
• India remains relative bright spot for demand resilience  
• AI vs consumer electronics = ongoing capacity competition  

Impact: Negative for consumer electronics (cost inflation) | Positive for memory chip suppliers 📈

GREAVES COTTON : ELECTRIC MOBILITY LAUNCHES AMPERE MAGNUS NEO — NEW VARIANT AT Rs 86,999 ⚡🛵🇮🇳

• 🛵 Launch: All-new Magnus Neo under Ampere brand — lighter build (103 kg kerb weight), lower seat height (777 mm), 10-inch rear tyre for better city handling & comfort

• 🔋 Tech: LFP battery with 2x extra battery life cycle — up to 10-year battery life; tested across -40°C to 60°C; dual-frame chassis

• 💰 Price: Starting at Rs 86,999 | Available in 4 colours — Mystic Mauve, Butter Yellow, Ocean Blue & Matcha Green

• 📋 IPO Note: GEML has filed DRHP with SEBI (Dec 2024) — IPO pending regulatory approvals

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Mildly Positive
Product refresh on an existing platform — incremental rather than transformative; competitive pricing targets mass urban commuter segment; real catalyst remains GEML's pending IPO which will be the key value unlocking event for Greaves Cotton shareholders

CHALET HOTELS ACQUIRES INDER RESIDENCY RESORT & SPA, UDAIPUR FOR Rs 171 Cr 🏨🌅🇮🇳

• 🎯 Deal: 100% acquisition of Seasons Hotels Pvt Ltd — owner of Inder Residency Resort & Spa, Udaipur | 144 rooms | 8.2 acres | Cash consideration of Rs 171 Cr | Close by May 15, 2026

• 🏗️ Upgrade Plan: Property to be repositioned as upper upscale / premium lifestyle destination — will remain non-operative during refurbishment; room expansion also being evaluated

• 📊 Asset Financials: Target turnover Rs 7.43 Cr (FY25) vs Rs 10.35 Cr (FY24) — revenue declined, signalling underperformance that CHL aims to unlock via repositioning

• 🗺️ Strategic Fit: CHL's first foray into Rajasthan — diversifies portfolio into high-growth leisure & wedding/MICE market; aligns with Lakshya-style leisure expansion strategy

🟢 Impact: Positive
Udaipur is one of India's top leisure & destination wedding markets — entry at Rs 171 Cr for a 8.2-acre resort is reasonable; significant value creation potential via refurbishment & brand upgrade; near-term revenue impact is nil (non-operative during renovation) but medium-term re-rating possible once repositioned

text:
TESLA – CAPEX SURGE & STRATEGIC PIVOT 🚗🤖📊

CAPEX EXPANSION  
• 2026 capex guided at >$25 billion  
• ~3x increase vs last year  
• Raised from earlier ~$20 billion forecast  
• Management signals “very significant increase” in spending  

STRATEGIC SHIFT  
• Transition from EV-focused company → AI & robotics platform  
• Core focus areas: AI, autonomous driving, robotics, mobility  
• Marks a major evolution in Tesla’s long-term vision  

INVESTMENT AREAS  
• Expansion of global factory operations  
• Optimus humanoid robot production  
• AI infrastructure & model development  
• Cybercab (robotaxi) autonomous vehicle platform  

WHY THE PIVOT  
• Core EV business under pressure for ~2 years  
• Slowing growth + rising competition  
• AI/robotics seen as next growth engine  

MARKET REACTION  
• Stock fell ~3.2% post announcement  
• After-hours gains erased  
• Investor concerns over rising cash burn  

ANALYST VIEW  
• Heavy capex to compress near-term free cash flow  
• “Reality check” on FCF expectations (Dec Mullarkey)  
• Shift from narrative optimism → financial scrutiny  

STRATEGIC BET  
• Aim: leadership in humanoid robotics + autonomous mobility  
• Competing against Nvidia, Boston Dynamics, Figure AI, Waymo  
• First-mover scale advantage critical  

BUSINESS DYNAMICS  
• Auto segment funding AI/robotics transition  
• Increasing capital intensity across verticals  
• Higher execution dependency vs past  

RISK FACTORS  
• Elevated capex → FCF pressure  
• Execution risk in unproven segments (Optimus, robotaxi)  
• Competitive intensity in AI & autonomy  
• Balance sheet stretch if returns delayed  

CORE INSIGHT  
• Classic high-risk, high-reward Musk strategy  
• Tesla doubling down on next-gen platforms before EV moat weakens  
• Outcome binary: platform leader vs capital-heavy EV player  
• Execution now the key variable for valuation  

Impact: Neutral to Negative near-term (FCF pressure) | Positive long-term if execution succeeds 🚀

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Mildly Positive

text:
IKS HEALTH – TRUBRIDGE ACQUISITION 🚑💻📊

DEAL OVERVIEW  
• IKS Health to acquire US-based TruBridge Inc. for up to $565 million  
• Shareholders to receive $26.25 per share in cash  
• Deal expected to close by Q3 2026  

FUNDING STRUCTURE  
• Financed via loan up to $670 million  
• Arranged by Citi, Deutsche Bank & JPMorgan  

STRATEGIC SIGNIFICANCE  
• Largest acquisition for the Mumbai-based healthcare tech firm  
• Expands footprint in US healthcare IT market  
• Strengthens global healthcare technology positioning  

TARGET PROFILE  
• TruBridge is a Nasdaq-listed company  
• Focused on rural & community hospitals in the US  
• Strong presence in healthcare IT & services  

BUSINESS EXPANSION  
• Entry into SaaS-based EHR (electronic health records) segment  
• Diversification into subscription-based healthcare software  
• Enhances recurring revenue visibility  

TECH & SYNERGIES  
• Combines AI + human expertise for healthcare delivery  
• Improves clinical & operational efficiency  
• Builds connected healthcare workflow ecosystem  

LONG-TERM STRATEGY  
• Scale-up in global healthcare tech solutions  
• Deepens presence in US healthcare ecosystem  
• Positions IKS as a comprehensive healthcare IT platform  

CORE INSIGHT  
• Transformational deal to shift towards platform + SaaS model  
• Strong push into high-margin, recurring revenue business  
• Focus on AI-led efficiency & integrated care delivery  

Impact: Positive for IKS Health’s global growth & SaaS transition 🚀

FLEX OFFICE LEASING SURGE – COWORKING GAINS SHARE 🏢📊

QUARTER HIGHLIGHTS  
• Coworking firms leased ~2 million sq ft in Q4 FY26  
• Among the largest deal closures in the quarter  
• Strong demand momentum continues  

TOP OPERATORS – DEAL ACTIVITY  
• Smartworks: 900,000 sq ft (Gurugram + Bengaluru)  
• WeWork: 575,000 sq ft (Mumbai, Chennai, Hyderabad)  
• The Executive Centre: 300,000 sq ft (Hyderabad + Bengaluru)  
• Simpliwork: 100,000 sq ft (Chennai)  
• Awfis: 24,000 sq ft (Kolkata)  
• Redbrick: 21,000 sq ft (Kolkata)  
• Table Space: among top players (listing-bound)  

WEWORK INDIA – KEY UPDATES  
• Signed >700,000 sq ft across major cities  
• 10-year lease (~81,000 sq ft) at Embassy Vertex, Bengaluru  
• Additional 2.6 lakh sq ft leased in Pune  
• Management highlights strong scale + evolving demand quality  

DEMAND DRIVERS  
• GCC (Global Capability Centres) expansion  
• Need for flexibility without compromising quality  
• Faster scaling with lower upfront capital  

ENTERPRISE SHIFT  
• Structural shift, not cyclical (Awfis management)  
• Flex now mainstream for large corporates  
• Converts fixed real estate costs → variable operating costs  
• Reduces time-to-market significantly  

COST ADVANTAGE  
• 71% of enterprises cite cost efficiency as key factor  
• Better capital allocation + operational agility  

PREMIUM SEGMENT TREND  
• Rise of ultra-luxury workspaces  
• Kepler by SKV leasing premium centres in Gurugram  
• Competing with The Executive Centre in high-end segment  

MARKET SHARE SHIFT  
• Flex accounts for >20% of total office absorption  
• Rapid share gains despite overall market slowdown  

INDUSTRY OUTLOOK  
• Flex penetration expected to reach 12–15% of total office stock  
• Enterprise-led demand to drive growth  

BROADER OFFICE MARKET  
• Q4 FY26 absorption: 11.51 million sq ft  
• ↓ 28% QoQ | ↓ 24% YoY  
• Impacted by slower leasing + supply constraints  

CORE INSIGHT  
• Flex space gaining share even as overall demand moderates  
• Smartworks + WeWork alone ~1.5 mn sq ft deals  
• GCC growth + cost optimisation = structural tailwind  
• Flex emerging as long-term winner in commercial real estate  

Impact: Positive for coworking / flex workspace players 📈

RENEW ENERGY – SOLAR MANUFACTURING EXPANSION ☀️🏭📊

PROJECT OVERVIEW  
• ReNew Energy to set up 6.5 GW ingot-wafer solar manufacturing facility  
• Investment: ₹4,200 crore (~$450 million)  
• Location: Anakapalli (near Vizag, Andhra Pradesh)  

STRATEGIC SIGNIFICANCE  
• Among India’s first large-scale ingot-wafer facilities  
• Strengthens domestic solar supply chain  
• Reduces dependence on imports  

CAPACITY & TIMELINE  
• Planned capacity: 6.5 GW  
• Expected commissioning in ~24 months  
• Large-scale integrated manufacturing setup  

BACKWARD INTEGRATION  
• Enables control over key upstream components (ingots & wafers)  
• Supports solar PV modules & cell manufacturing  
• Improves cost efficiency & supply security  

POLICY SUPPORT  
• Boost from ALMM inclusion of wafers & ingots (effective June 2028)  
• Strong alignment with government’s localization push  

EMPLOYMENT IMPACT  
• ~2,100 direct & indirect jobs to be created  
• Supports regional industrial development  

BROADER INVESTMENT CONTEXT  
• Part of ₹82,000 crore clean energy investment plan in Andhra Pradesh  
• Reinforces state’s renewable energy ambitions  

INDUSTRY IMPACT  
• Strengthens India’s solar manufacturing ecosystem  
• Accelerates import substitution in solar value chain  
• Supports long-term energy transition goals  

CORE INSIGHT  
• Shift from module assembly → full value chain integration  
• Strategic move to capture higher margins & reduce supply risk  
• Andhra emerging as a clean energy manufacturing hub  

Impact: Positive for domestic solar manufacturing & energy transition 🌱📈

INDIA POWER DEMAND SURGE – GRID STRESS BUILDING ⚡️📊

DEMAND TREND  
• Peak demand already ~240 GW (Apr 1: 215.8 → Apr 22: 239.7 GW)  
• Near-record levels before peak summer  

HISTORICAL CONTEXT  
• All-time high: 250 GW (May 2024)  
• FY25 peak: 242.7 GW  
• New highs likely in FY26  

CEA FORECAST  
• Peak demand estimate: 271 GW  
• Implies record grid stress  

DRIVERS  
• Heatwave (+4–8°C above normal)  
• El Niño impact  
• Surge in AC/cooling demand  
• Industrial & commercial load growth  

SUPPLY SITUATION  
• Installed capacity: ~524 GW  
• FY26 additions: ~55 GW  
• Adequate capacity, but peak-hour tightness  

GRID REALITY  
• Coal remains base load backbone  
• Renewables intermittent  
• Gas peaker capacity limited  
• Hydro risk if monsoon weak  

DISCOM PREPAREDNESS  
• Power banking arrangements (e.g., BSES)  
• Inter-state tie-ups  
• Lower reliance on spot market  

KEY RISKS  
• Below-normal monsoon (~92%)  
• Hydro stress  
• Peak shortages  
• Possible tariff hikes  

CORE INSIGHT  
• Demand peaking early  
• Flexibility (storage + transmission) is the key bottleneck  
• Grid infra becomes critical  

Impact: Positive for power & transmission players ⚡️

TAJ GVK HOTELS – PROMOTER PLEDGE REDUCTION

• Taj GVK Hotels & Resorts Limited sees release of 56,03,120 pledged shares (8.94% of equity)
• Shares released by Moonshot Trust and Starlight Trust (28,01,560 each) on April 22, 2026

• Indicates improving financial position of promoter entities

text:
GOVT RESTRICTS SURPLUS AMMONIA SUPPLY TO FERTILISER SECTOR — INDIA PHARMA FACES RAW MATERIAL CRUNCH 💊⚗️🇮🇳

• 🚨 Trigger: Department of Fertilizers issues advisory restricting sale of surplus ammonia exclusively to the fertiliser sector — cutting off pharma industry access

• ⚗️ Critical Input: Ammonia is a key raw material for API (Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient) production — alternative sourcing options remain extremely limited

• 🏭 Industry Impact: Drugmakers warn of potential disruption to API manufacturing, possible medicine shortages & hit to pharmaceutical exports

• 🌍 Export Risk: India is the world's largest generic medicine supplier — any API production disruption has global downstream consequences

🔴 Impact: Negative for Pharma Sector
A policy decision intended to support fertiliser availability creates an unintended supply chain threat for one of India's most critical export industries; API-focused companies most at risk — watch for sector-level representations to government for exemptions or alternative allocation mechanisms; near-term negative for API manufacturers and pharma cos with high ammonia dependency

text:
INDIA – STRUCTURAL RISKS (BERNSTEIN VIEW)

• Bernstein flags key long-term structural challenges for India

🌾 Agriculture Drag
• ~42–45% workforce vs ~15–16% GDP contribution
• Challenges: small land holdings, monsoon dependence, subsidy-heavy system
• Subsidy burden ~₹3–4 lakh Cr; need shift toward income-based support

⚡️ Energy Gap
• Execution lag in power sector vs targets
• Discom losses estimated at ₹5–6 lakh Cr
• Industrial cross-subsidisation continues
• Need to reduce oil imports and accelerate clean energy transition

🤖 AI Risk
• Risk of India being a “passive consumer” in global AI ecosystem
• Strong data centre push but limited domestic frontier models
• IT & BPO sectors face disruption risk
• Gaps in compute infrastructure, data ecosystem, and AI capabilities

🏭 Manufacturing Challenge
• Manufacturing share stagnant at ~16–17% of GDP
• Continued reliance on imports (EV batteries, critical components)
• PLI schemes supportive but deeper ecosystem gaps remain
• Need focus on robotics, advanced materials, and AI-led manufacturing

Impact: Mixed to Negative (Long-term) – structural gaps may constrain growth if unaddressed, though they also highlight key policy and investment opportunities across sectors

text:
GUJARAT GAS REVIVES MORBI CERAMIC CLUSTER WITH ASSURED SUPPLY & STABLE PRICING 🔥🏺🇮🇳

• 🌍 Crisis: Geopolitical disruptions triggered raw material cost surge, propane restrictions & freight inflation — 2 lakh+ workers hit as Morbi ceramic units shut from mid-March 2026

• 📉 Volume Crash: GGL's Morbi gas consumption collapsed to 0.36 mmscmd (83 units) as of 31st March 2026 — from peak levels of ~6–7 mmscmd

• 🛡️ GGL Response: Proactively sourced gas from non-Middle East spot markets; engaged Morbi Ceramic Association; offered competitive & stable pricing with full supply assurance for May 2026

• 📈 Sharp Recovery: Volumes rebounded to 2.70 mmscmd (290 units) by 22nd April — a 7.5x surge in 3 weeks

• 🎯 May 2026 Target: 675–700 active units with total consumption of 6–7 mmscmd — near full normalisation expected

🟢 Impact: Positive
Morbi is GGL's largest industrial cluster — the steep volume recovery trajectory is a strong positive read-through for Q1FY27 earnings; price stability commitment deepens customer stickiness; supply reliability in crisis reinforces GGL's moat as the authorised PNG distributor — Q4FY26 (March quarter) remains a drag, but May recovery largely offsets the loss

text:
CITI on Dr Reddys

Sell, TP Rs 1070

DRL’s stock surged 9% yesterday on unconfirmed reports of potential generic Semaglutide approval in Canada, a development that, if confirmed, would be positive given co’s past regulatory hurdles in Canada/Brazil.

However, believe stock movement overstates any potential upside, as maintain FY28E product revenue expectation of US$50m in a six-player market

While FY27E revenues could reach US$80-100m (vs current US$60m estimate) in a three-player market (DRL, Sandoz, Apotex) with a mandatory 65% generic discount, significant upside to US$150-180m appears improbable, requiring both an absence of competition (low probability given Apotex’s US approval) and no innovator price cuts

With 4Q FY26E earnings expected to reflect ex-Revlimid numbers, expect downward revision in Street earnings est.

All this happened when markets had many phases of volatility & corrections. These 2 stories are more about business strength than market mood.
I had spoken about both earlier in my tweets (multiple times since 2022) & am happy to share how & why it actually played out so well ! 

GE Vernova saw a strong T&D cycle kick in. Order inflows improved, execution picked up, margins expanded. Being a leader in T&D Technology was it's biggest stretch and the market started pricing future visibility instead of current earnings (which were improving too).

Shilchar Tech was more niche but a very agressive bet at the time in the transformers space. with  strong demand, tight supply, solid margins & clean (one of the best in this domain) balance sheet. Smaller base, but consistent execution led to steady compounding & I still feel it is 1 of the best transformer story in Indi with export tailwinds. 

Point to note here is that in both cases, earnings kept delivering, balance sheets stayed clean & sector tailwinds helped too. 
Going forward I believe there is still a LOT OF STEAM left in these great #multibaggers ! 💯
BREAKING: Trump says Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by three weeks

GE Vernova has been my long term favorite in the T&D space. Had mentioned it a few times earlier too & also shared more insights in my stock analysis articles. Am just sharing what played out, so so well ! Strong business & stronger execution. Stock has doubled again. A reminder that high P/E with growth can still deliver real returns over time ! SARLA PERFORMANCE FIBERS DIVESTS US SUBSIDIARY SARLAFLEX INC.; BOOKS ₹7,713 LAKH LOSS ON LEGACY INVESTMENT 🇺🇸📉🧹

• 💸 SPFL divests preference shares in Sarlaflex Inc. (USA WOS, operations suspended since Dec 2017) for sale consideration of just ₹111.59 Lakhs; recognizes a loss of ₹7,713.26 Lakhs — largely a non-cash accounting write-off with no impact on operational liquidity

• 📊 Original investment ~₹100 Cr (equity + loans converted to preference shares); subsidiary had negative net worth as of March 31, 2026; exit removes dead capital from balance sheet and expected to improve ROCE

• 🔭 Management optimistic on revenue growth via export expansion & new customer additions in current year

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Mildly Positive (Large headline loss is predominantly non-cash; strategically the right call — cleaning up a long-dormant, loss-making foreign subsidiary improves balance sheet quality and ROCE optics; operational business outlook remains intact with new customer wins)

Morepen Laboratories Limited – FUNDING UPDATE

💵 New Loan Facility

• Finance Committee approved ₹30 Cr unsecured term loan from Shinhan Bank

• Purpose: General business requirements

📊 Loan Terms

• Interest rate: Repo (5.25%) + 2.35% = ~7.60% p.a. (floating)

• Tenure: 36 months

• Repayment: 10 equal quarterly instalments

• Moratorium: 6 months

🔐 Security Structure

• No asset collateral provided

• Backed only by personal guarantee of Promoter, Chairman & MD Sushil Suri

📉 Debt Position Context

• Existing outstanding debt (before this loan): ₹182.62 Cr

• Existing Shinhan Bank exposure: ₹20.45 Cr

• Post sanction total Shinhan exposure: ~₹50.45 Cr

• Overall debt rises to ~₹212+ Cr (post drawdown)

📊 Assessment

• Routine corporate borrowing to support operations

• Unsecured nature increases reliance on promoter guarantee

• Floating rate exposes company to interest rate movements

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Mildly Negative – While funding is routine, rising leverage and unsecured structure backed only by promoter guarantee warrant monitoring, especially given increasing total debt exposure and floating-rate interest risk

RIKHAV SECURITIES: PROMOTER GROUP ANNOUNCES OPEN OFFER

Move reflects strong promoter confidence in company’s fundamentals, growth visibility and long term business outlook

Public shareholders have the option to tender their shares in the open offer as per SEBI prescribed process and timelines

VIPUL LIMITED GETS NCLT APPROVAL FOR MERGER OF 5 WHOLLY OWNED SUBSIDIARIES; SCHEME EFFECTIVE FROM APRIL 1, 2022 ⚖️🏢✅

• 🏛️ NCLT New Delhi (Court-II) passes order on April 17, 2026 sanctioning amalgamation of 5 WOS — Abhipra Trading, Graphic Research Consultants, United Buildwell, Vineeta Trading & Vipul Eastern Infracon — into Vipul Limited; appointed date: April 1, 2022

• ⚠️ Key condition — Scheme effective only after payment of unstayed income tax dues; Vipul Ltd carries a significant IT demand of ₹27.66 Cr and accumulated losses of ₹194.79 Cr (as of March 2023); all liabilities of transferor companies to be absorbed by Vipul Ltd

• 📋 No loss carry-forward benefit permitted; IT/GST authorities retain full rights to pursue tax proceedings against the merged entity

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Mildly Positive (Structural simplification and compliance rationalization are positives; however, large pending tax demands, accumulated losses and the back-dated appointed date add near-term liability and compliance complexity for the merged entity)

ETHANOL BLENDING – INDUSTRY OUTLOOK

• Government accelerating push toward E100 fuel and flex-fuel vehicles
• At 30% blending, ethanol demand estimated at ~15 bn litres (industry commentary)
• Current industry capacity also around ~15 bn litres

• Near-term view suggests adequate to excess capacity in the system
• Higher blending targets expected to absorb surplus over time

• Volume growth likely across ethanol producers as blending increases
• Policy push supports long-term demand visibility for sugar/ethanol players

• Flex-fuel ecosystem development to further strengthen ethanol adoption

Impact: Positive – higher blending targets drive volume growth and improve capacity utilization for ethanol producers, though near-term oversupply may cap pricing upside

VARDHMAN TEXTILES ACQUIRES 31.2% STAKE IN RENEW GREEN (MPR FOUR) FOR CAPTIVE WIND-SOLAR HYBRID POWER 🌬️☀️⚡

• 🤝 Vardhman Textiles signs Share Subscription & Shareholders' Agreement with ReNew Green Energy Solutions Pvt. Ltd. (Promoter) and ReNew Green (MPR Four) Pvt. Ltd. (Power Producer), along with a Captive Power Purchase Agreement — all executed on April 23, 2026

• 🏭 Project — 19 MW AC Wind-Solar Hybrid Power Plant (26.4 MW wind + 15 MW solar capacity) at District Ratlam, Madhya Pradesh; power to be supplied exclusively to Vardhman Textiles under Captive Rules; operated by ReNew Green (MPR Four)

• 💰 Vardhman's equity contribution: ₹24.29 Cr for 31.2% stake in ReNew Green (MPR Four) Pvt. Ltd. (total project capital ~₹77.86 Cr); payment to be made in 3 tranches based on project completion stages; no board nominee appointment by Vardhman

• 📋 ReNew Green (MPR Four) is a SPV incorporated on June 21, 2022 under Companies Act, 2013; yet to commence commercial operations; no regulatory approvals required for acquisition

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strategically sound move — secures long-term captive renewable energy supply, supporting energy cost reduction and sustainability goals for a power-intensive textile business; asset-light structure with phased investment limits upfront cash outflow; aligns with India's green energy transition push)

text:
BERNSTEIN INDIA STRATEGY NOTE – HARD QUESTIONS FOR POLICY ⚠️

EMPLOYMENT RISK  
• IT/BPO jobs under GenAI threat  
• 10–15 Mn workforce exposed  
• Manufacturing absorption limited  
• Quality jobs vs gig work debate  

AGRICULTURE CHALLENGE  
• 42–45% workforce, ~16% GDP  
• Small landholdings, monsoon risk  
• Subsidy-heavy model persists  
• Need logistics, income transfer shift  

AI STRATEGY GAP  
• Risk: AI consumer, not creator  
• No frontier AI models  
• Data value flowing abroad  
• Need domestic models, compute  

MANUFACTURING REALITY  
• GDP share stuck ~16–17%  
• PLI momentum, but shallow depth  
• Import dependence (e.g., batteries)  
• Need early bets: robotics, materials  

FISCAL TRADE-OFF  
• Cash transfers ₹1.7–2.5T  
• ~0.5% of GDP  
• Crowding out capex risk  
• Consumption vs investment debate  

R&D CONSTRAINT  
• Spend ~0.6–0.7% of GDP  
• Low for deep-tech ambitions  
• Talent pipeline concerns  
• Funding gap in innovation  

CORE INSIGHT  
• Structural issues, not cyclical  
• Policy choices critical now  
• Window open, but narrowing  

Impact: Long-term structural policy debate

Dividend yield: 8–12% per year (often 9–9.5% after tax)

Almost 90% is roads + telecom. New money is going to green energy and warehouses – this is the fresh growth area

GOLDMAN SACHS – OIL MARKET OUTLOOK

• Goldman Sachs expects capex to return to the oil sector
• Peak oil demand timeline shifting from ~2020 to late 2040s

• Brent crude forecast at ~$80/bbl by year-end
• In case of ~30-day Strait of Hormuz disruption, prices could rise to $90–100/bbl

• Downstream pressure expected on diesel, jet fuel, and naphtha cracks
• Refining margins likely to remain under stress
• Naphtha highlighted as key petrochemical feedstock

• ~45% of global naphtha exports originate from West Asia
• Supply disruptions could tighten feedstock availability

• Higher crude + weaker cracks = margin squeeze for refiners
• Petrochemical companies may face input cost inflation

Impact: Mixed – positive for upstream oil producers due to higher crude prices, but negative for refiners and petrochemical players due to margin pressure

BALRAMPUR CHINI MILLS — BOARD APPROVES ₹450 CR PREFERENTIAL ISSUE, ₹200 CR NCD, PLA CAPEX REVISION & NEW GYPSUM PLANT 🏭💰✅

• 💰 Preferential Issue of ~93.17 lakh equity shares at ₹483/share aggregating ₹450 Cr to 11 allottees — promoters (Vivek Saraogi, Sumedha Saraogi, Meenakshi Mercantiles) and non-promoters including Tata Small Cap Fund, 360 One Pipe Fund, Alchemy & ICICI Prudential AIFs; EGM called for May 20, 2026

• 📋 NCD issuance of up to ₹200 Cr on private placement basis approved (enabling resolution); terms to be disclosed at time of allotment

• 🏗️ PLA Project (80,000 TPA) capex revised upward by ₹230 Cr to ₹3,080 Cr (from ₹2,850 Cr) due to construction cost inflation, supply chain disruptions and engineering design changes; funded via preferential proceeds, debt and internal accruals

• 🧱 New Lactogypsum Processing Plant approved at Kumbhi, UP — capacity of 76 lakh gypsum boards/annum, capex ₹160 Cr, commercial production targeted by December 2027; to be funded from preferential issue proceeds

Robust HVDC Pipeline in the Offing

According to the National Electricity Plan for Transmission, the CEA aims to scale the current HVDC operational capacity from 33.5 GW to 66.75 GW in FY32.

For this purpose,8-9 projects are being planned at a a total cost of ~ Rs 1,90,000 crore till FY32. Going beyond FY32, additional 6-7 projects are being planned with a Rs 1,00,000 crore investment.

HVDC theme is not a 1-2 year theme. Doesn't mean one should chase at any price. There will be intermittent corrections and that might be the opportunity for those who are not playing the theme.

ENVIRO INFRA ENGINEERS – STRATEGIC ACQUISITION

• Through subsidiary EIE Renewables Pvt Ltd, acquires 49% stake in PRA Bihar BESS Pvt Ltd

Elecon Engineering Company – Q4 FY26 CONCALL HIGHLIGHTS

• Q4FY26 concall held on April 16, 2026
• Performance impacted by slower order conversion and customer capex recalibration

Financial Highlights (Q4 FY26)

• Consolidated Revenue: ₹746 Cr (vs ₹798 Cr YoY)
• Impacted by delayed order pipeline conversion and dispatch timelines

• Gear Division: ₹472 Cr (63% mix, ↓ 21% YoY)
→ Weakness due to delayed order inflows and customer deferments
→ Underlying demand remains intact

• MHE Division: ₹274 Cr (37% mix, ↑ 37% YoY)
→ Strong growth driven by demand from power, cement, and ports sectors

Key Insight
• Short-term revenue pressure due to execution delays, not demand destruction
• MHE segment emerging as strong growth driver

Bottom Line
• Near-term volatility in gear segment
• Medium-term outlook supported by industrial capex cycle and sector demand recovery

- No explicit numerical guidance, but directional growth confidence remains strong  
- 12-month order backlog provides execution visibility for next year  
- Impact: Positive — improving revenue visibility  

**MARGIN OUTLOOK**  
- EBITDA margin expected to remain stable at ~16%–16.1%  
- Supported by:  
  • Operational efficiencies  
  • AI-driven productivity gains  
  • Pricing discipline in competitive markets  
- Impact: Neutral to Positive — stability over expansion  

**DEMAND ENVIRONMENT & GEOGRAPHY**  
- North America expected to return to growth with improving lead indicators  
- AMEA region also likely to shift towards growth (subject to geopolitical risks)  
- Impact: Positive — broad-based recovery potential  

**OPERATING METRICS**  
- Headcount growth slower than revenue growth  
- Expected increase in revenue per employee (productivity gains)  
- Impact: Positive — margin-supportive efficiency  

**ORDER BOOK & EXECUTION**  
- Strong 12-month backlog expected to be executed within FY27  
- Provides better revenue base vs FY26  
- Impact: Positive — higher predictability  

**RISKS & CONCERNS**  
- Continued pricing pressure in competitive IT services market  
- Dependence on macro recovery in key geographies  
- Geopolitical uncertainties impacting AMEA region  
- Impact: Monitorable  

**KEY INSIGHT**  
- Story is shifting from margin expansion to stable margins with productivity-led growth and improving execution visibility  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Gradual growth recovery supported by order backlog and regional improvement  
- Margins expected to remain stable rather than expand significantly  

**IMPACT**  
- Positive — steady execution with improving demand outlook, though limited margin upside

STOCKS IN FOCUS

Century Ply, Greenply, Greenpanel and Rushil Décor in focus

Majority of the larger MDF manufacturers have taken a price hike of 7-10% effective from mid-April 26 onwards

text:
Avendus on Indian Alcoholic Beverages — Sector Initiation
*Premiumization Drives Value-Led Growth | Broad-Based Buy Across Coverage*

**Sector Thesis**
- Alcohol: One of India's most resilient consumption categories
- Demand anchored in habit, social occasions, and rising aspiration
- Strong entry barriers via state regulation, high taxation, controlled distribution
- Premiumization shifting industry from volume story to value-led growth
- Better brands and experiences driving consumer upgrades across segments
- Stronger margin potential as premium mix rises across portfolios

**Key Sector Characteristics**
- Heavy state-level regulation creates natural competitive moats
- Controlled route-to-market structures limit new entrant disruption
- High taxation embeds pricing power for established brand owners
- Category resilience tested across cycles — demand remains sticky

**Ratings & Target Prices**
- United Spirits (USL): Buy | TP ₹1,662
- Radico Khaitan: Buy | TP ₹4,177
- Tilaknagar Industries: Buy | TP ₹550
- Allied Blenders & Distillers: Buy | TP ₹671
- United Breweries: Add | TP ₹1,837

**Avendus Investment Stance**
- Broad-based initiations signal high conviction on sector re-rating
- Premiumization theme central to all five coverage additions
- Four outright Buys; United Breweries initiated with Add
- Value-led growth with margin expansion seen as multi-year theme
- Regulatory moats and brand loyalty underpin long-term investor interest

JPMorgan on Yes Bank — Q4 FY26 Brokerage Note
Underweight Maintained | Valuation Limits Upside Despite Beat

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Underweight (UW) maintained
- Target Price: ₹18 per share

**Q4 FY26 Result Snapshot**
- PAT: ₹10.7 billion, +45% YoY, +12% QoQ
- PAT came 9% above JPMorgan estimates — a clear beat
- Opex rose just 2% YoY; came 2% below JPMe — tight cost control
- Credit costs at 28bps; 4bps lower than JPMe

**Key Positives**
- First time RoA hit 1% since bank reconstruction
- Cost-to-Income Ratio (CIR) improved to 63% during the quarter
- Credit costs remained contained at 28bps vs. JPMe of 32bps
- Operating leverage visible with opex growth well below revenue growth

**Key Concerns**
- RoA of 1% supported by 31bps contribution from SR (Stressed Asset) recoveries
- Sustainability of 1% RoA without SR tailwinds remains unproven
- CIR at 63% remains elevated vs. peers; continues to weigh on RoA
- MSME portfolio at risk from supply chain disruptions
- Credit costs likely to inch higher as MSME stress emerges

**Valuation View**
- Stock trading at 1.2x P/B FY27E
- JPM believes current valuation already prices in balance sheet growth
- RoA improvement potential also largely baked into price
- Limited re-rating catalyst visible at current levels

**JPM Investment Stance**
- Beat acknowledged but structural concerns persist
- SR recovery-driven RoA not a sustainable earnings quality signal
- Underweight stance retained; TP ₹18 implies limited upside
- Key monitorable: Visibility on RoA sustainability ex-SR recoveries
Motilal Oswal on Angel One: Maintain buy with target price of ₹400 vs ₹300

Nomura on Guj Gas

Buy TP Rs 390

GSPC-GGL-GSPL amalgamation gets MCA approval

Several positives from amalgamation; share price may rerate

Believe amalgamation & subsequent demerger of transmission business makes good strategic sense along with some immediate economic gains for the merged entity:

1) simpler group structure as it eliminates cross holdings;

2) integrated gas value chain creates scale and efficiency;

3) valuation discount due to conglomerate structure and cross holding goes away;

4) earnings for CGD entity may increase due to reduction in marketing margins currently being accrued to GSPC,

5) accumulated tax losses of INR72bn on GSPC books which can be used by the merged entity to offset future tax liabilities.

Overall, believe merger is EPS-accretive on an immediate basis

Investec on JSW Steel

Buy, TP Rs 1525

JSTL’s proposed BPSL monetization coupled with a strong OCF/FCF profile over FY27-28E points to a materially de-levered balance sheet.

Management’s clear focus on optimized capital structure suggests to us ‘more’ growth capex.

Post Utkal (5MT, Q3FY26 announcement), JSTL has now announced a 6 MT JV with POSCO, likely driven by access to technology and a more efficient operating model

Highlight JSTL’s low-cost brownfield optionality of 18 MT+ (including BPSL), underpinning superior incremental RoCE

PAT beat (↓ provisions), loan growth ~12% YoY, LDR ↓ to 94.6%; RoE ~14–15%

UBS on HDFC BK

Buy, TP Rs 1175

Q4 PAT beat driven by lower provisions

Loans grew c12% YoY; LDR down c400bp QoQ to 94.6%

Management expects loan growth momentum to sustain

Expect c14-15% ROE in FY27-28E

CLSA on HDFC BK

O-P, TP Rs 1200

4QFY26 NII and PPOP were largely in line, while PBT was a 4% beat

Would characterise it as a steady performance in a slightly challenging quarter.

Loan growth of 12% YoY was largely in line

Sequential quarterly-average (QA) deposit growth picked up from the past couple of quarters.

LDR (a metric we consider irrelevant, but many investors still find important) declined to 95% from 99% in prior quarter.

Margins were up 3bp QoQ, a decent outcome in this environment when cost of wholesale deposits rose sharply post the Gulf conflict.

HDFCB’s gross slippages have declined YoY on an absolute basis for three consecutive quarters.

JPM on HDFC BK

OW, TP Rs 990

Delivered a steady 4Q26 with PAT broadly in line vs JPMe.

While NII (-4% vs JPMe) and NIMs trailed JPMe on elevated competitive intensity and an adverse product mix, they were offset by lower than expected loan loss provisions.

NII growth for HDFCB has remained volatile, decelerating to 3.2% y/y in 4Q26 vs 4.8%/6.4% in 2Q/3Q, respectively

Expect NII growth to accelerate in FY27/28 on a better-than-expected pick-up in system credit growth and funding cost benefits from a decline in the share of higher cost borrowings.

Decline in average LCR to 114% (vs 116% in 3Q, 110-120% target range) does reduce balance-sheet flexibility to support margins/RoA in FY27
